title: ruellia lactea var. acaulis
intro: Ruellia lactea var. acaulis, commonly known as the white ruellia, is a flowering plant species in the Acanthaceae family. It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, contributing to various health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive relief. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts can be used to make decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which can be applied topically or taken orally to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
